title: doctor offers to restart the running herdr-go service after a real web-token fix
action: In src/doctor/checks.rs: after a real (non-no-op) web-token fix, offer to restart the actually-running herdr-go service so the new token takes effect. Add active_service_restart() -> Option<(&'static str label, restart closure)>: Linux branch reuses systemd_state("herdr-go.service") (checks.rs:274) and treats it active only when the returned state == "active"; restart runs `systemctl --user restart herdr-go.service`. macOS branch resolves uid via `id -u` (Command::new("id").arg("-u")), builds target = format!("gui/{uid}/io.github.vantt.herdr-go"), probes with `launchctl print <target>` (success => active), restart runs `launchctl kickstart -k <target>`. No active service on either branch (including Windows, where neither systemctl nor launchctl exist) -> None, no cfg guard needed -- matches the existing herdr_version()/systemd_state() graceful-degrade shape. Add offer_service_restart(reader, writer) as a thin public wrapper delegating to an injectable offer_service_restart_with(reader, writer, detect_fn) core (mirror the offer_web_token_fix / offer_web_token_fix_with split at checks.rs:544-599 exactly): if detect_fn() returns None, do nothing and return Ok(()) (silent skip, zero writer output, zero prompt); if Some((label, restart)), call prompt::confirm(reader, writer, &format!("{label} is running -- restart it now so the new token takes effect?"), true) and only call restart() on yes, reporting success/failure/error through writer -- never restart without that explicit yes. Wire it into the existing offer_fixes "web token" match arm (checks.rs ~330): call offer_service_restart(reader, writer) only when offer_web_token_fix(reader, writer)? returns true (a token was actually freshly generated) -- never when the check was already-satisfied (no-op). Do not touch doctor/mod.rs: offer_fixes' public signature is unchanged, and mod.rs:37-53 already gates the entire interactive phase 2 (including this new call) out for --check and non-TTY runs, so no second gate is needed here. No Cargo.toml changes -- std::process::Command only.

truths: offer_service_restart is invoked from the "web token" arm only when offer_web_token_fix(...)? returned true -- never on the already-available no-op path
detect_fn() == None -> Ok(()) with zero writer output and zero prompt call (silent skip)
an active service always requires an explicit prompt::confirm yes before restarting -- no automatic or silent restart under any path
Linux probe: systemd_state("herdr-go.service") must equal Some("active") (not merely Some(_)) before treating it as running; restart command is exactly `systemctl --user restart herdr-go.service`
macOS probe: uid resolved via `id -u`, target = gui/<uid>/io.github.vantt.herdr-go, checked via `launchctl print <target>` success; restart command is exactly `launchctl kickstart -k <target>`
no new Cargo.toml dependency; only std::process::Command, matching herdr_version()/systemd_state()'s existing style
unit tests exercise only the injectable offer_service_restart_with core with a fake detect closure -- never the real active_service_restart(), which would shell out to real systemctl/launchctl
doctor/mod.rs is unmodified; offer_fixes keeps its existing io::Result<()> signature
key_links: src/doctor/checks.rs:544-599 offer_web_token_fix / offer_web_token_fix_with pattern to mirror
src/doctor/mod.rs:37-53 existing --check/non-TTY gate that offer_fixes (and this new call) already inherits
prohibitions: no restart offered or triggered when --check is set or stdin/stderr is not a TTY
no automatic/silent restart under any circumstance
no new crate dependency added to Cargo.toml
no change to doctor/mod.rs or to offer_fixes' public signature
no unit test that shells out to the real systemctl or launchctl binaries

verify: cargo test --quiet doctor:: && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings

trace.outcome: Added active_service_restart() (systemd on Linux via existing systemd_state(), launchd on macOS via id -u + launchctl print/kickstart, no cfg guard needed) and offer_service_restart()/offer_service_restart_with(), wired into offer_fixes' web-token arm via a match guard so a restart is only offered after a freshly-generated token (never on the no-op path); confirm-gated, silent no-op when nothing is running.
